title: DID Document conformance fixture (ADR-125 / ADR-074 §D2′ — Multikey single form)
description: Envelope schema for did:nostr DID-document conformance vectors. The canonical document (valid:true vectors) is the ADR-125 §2 single form: two-context [https://www.w3.org/ns/cid/v1, https://w3id.org/nostr/context] (cid/v1 per updated did:nostr CG spec https://nostrcg.github.io/did-nostr/), top-level type 'DIDNostr', a single 'Multikey' verificationMethod whose publicKeyMultibase matches ^fe70102[0-9a-f]{64}$ and equals 'fe70102'+the DID-body hex (I2). The multibase==id-body, controller==id, lowercase-hex, and 71-char-length assertions are conformance-logic enforced by the harness per the vector's `valid` flag; this schema validates structure only (so negative documents still load and parse).

tier.description: Fixture-organisation label only. ADR-125 §D4′ collapses the Tier-1/Tier-3 split — the document shape is identical; service[] may be empty (minimal) or populated (production).

document.comment: Canonical (valid:true) documents additionally REQUIRE a top-level 'type':'DIDNostr' (ADR-125 §2 / D13′). 'type' is intentionally NOT in this envelope-level `required` list so that negative vectors carrying the superseded 2019 shape (which has no top-level type) still load and parse — the 'type'=='DIDNostr' requirement is conformance logic the harness enforces per the vector's `valid` flag, not envelope structure.

type.description: Top-level document type. Canonical (valid:true) value is REQUIRED and equals 'DIDNostr' (ADR-125 §2 / D13′). Absent on superseded 2019-shape negative vectors.
Canonical (valid:true) value is 'Multikey' (ADR-125 D13′). Negative vectors may carry the superseded 'SchnorrSecp256k1VerificationKey2019'.

publicKeyMultibase.description: Canonical (valid:true) value matches ^fe70102[0-9a-f]{64}$ and equals 'fe70102'+document.id-body (I2 round-trip). Harness-enforced per `valid`.

publicKeyHex.description: REMOVED from the canonical form (ADR-125 §2.2). Present only on superseded negative vectors.
